explanation: Material export requirements and edge cases remain unresolved before implementation, so the discussion workflow is the best fit.
The request is explicitly to settle caching semantics and trade-offs before implementation.
Permissions, failure semantics, idempotency, and the response contract are material unresolved requirements that need discussion first.
A rough feature idea needs a new top-level phased implementation plan with risks and validation.
The early dashboard idea needs an overall implementation plan covering constraints, milestones, risks, and success criteria.
The request is for a new top-level migration plan with phased rollout, rollback, testing, and risks.
An existing plan and named phase must be implemented, verified, and tracked.
The task is to refine and review an existing phase checklist for executable, verifiable completion.
The approved plan must be continued task by task with implementation, checks, and status tracking.
An approved PRD must be converted into ordered implementation issue drafts with the requested delivery details.
The existing implementation plan must become dependency-ordered issue drafts without external tracker mutation.
A finished plan must be decomposed into independently verifiable vertical-slice implementation issues.
The endpoint requirements are agreed and the task asks for implementation, focused tests, checks, and final review.
This is an ordinary behavior-preserving refactor with regression tests and executable checks.
The cause and fix are already agreed, so implementation with a focused regression test is appropriate.
The numerical solver change requires explicit tolerances, units, benchmark fixtures, and scientific invariant checks.
The stochastic scientific change needs test-first implementation, seed reproducibility, and distribution-level verification.
The sediment-flux implementation is domain-sensitive numerical code requiring units, conservation checks, benchmarks, and tolerances.
The request does not say whether the sharing feature needs requirements discussion, planning, or implementation.
The request combines creation of a new plan with immediate implementation without enough detail to choose the correct starting workflow.
An existing plan must be reviewed and refined into an executable, verifiable phase checklist.
A newly created repository needs first-time discovery, setup questions, constraints, and workflow routing.
The inherited repository is unfamiliar and lacks mature handoff context, so first-time project onboarding applies.
A new modelling project needs initial goals, constraints, repository context, checks, and workflow routing before implementation.
A mature repository must be resumed from its handoff, diary, and Git state with orientation before editing.
An established project must be reconstructed from instructions, diary, handoff, and worktree after compaction.
The request is to resume a mature repository by reconciling its handoff, diary, and Git state before continuing.
The requested deliverable is a deep single-page HTML state-of-play report spanning goals, progress, health, risks, changes, and next steps.
An evidence-backed HTML project-health report is needed for steering across goals, roadmap progress, performance, risks, and next actions.
The task asks for a progressively detailed one-page project status briefing covering all state-of-play dimensions.
Work is being paused and durable continuity notes are required for safe resumption by another agent.
The session is ending and needs a concise handoff of decisions, checks, unfinished work, risks, and next steps.
The work must be handed to a fresh session with repository state and a safe continuation sequence preserved.
The request gives no indication whether the project needs onboarding, resumption, status reporting, planning, or implementation.
The request combines a status report with continuing work but does not identify the mature context or existing plan needed to choose one workflow.
A newcomer needs neutral orientation to repository structure, runtime flow, dependencies, and change hotspots.
The request seeks neutral orientation to request flow, data transformations, storage, and relevant modules in an unfamiliar service.
An engineer needs a technical orientation to component responsibilities, dependencies, configuration, tests, and safe change locations.
The user explicitly requests an architectural critique of ownership, boundaries, coupling, duplication, and assumptions.
The request is to assess architectural scalability, dependency risks, bottlenecks, and refactoring opportunities without implementation.
Finding architectural duplication and misplaced responsibilities with prioritized remediation is an architecture review.
The user wants a teaching-oriented interactive HTML explanation of a pull request with diagrams, walkthrough, and quiz.
The task is a self-contained educational web explanation of a commit for a new team member.
A branch comparison must become an interactive HTML lesson with diagrams, examples, and a knowledge check.
The user explicitly wants concrete diff defects, regressions, missing tests, severity, and file references.
The pull request needs defect and risk review, test adequacy assessment, and concrete pre-merge fixes.
The current working-tree diff must be audited for concrete correctness, compatibility, and coverage findings.
An intermittent failure with unknown cause needs evidence-led reproduction and root-cause isolation before any fix.
Unexpected duplicate API records require root-cause investigation through logs, tests, and code paths before changes.
A newly crashing fixture needs regression timing, root-cause evidence, and a minimal reproduction before implementation edits.
Looking over changes could mean defect review or a teaching-oriented explanation, and the desired outcome is unspecified.
The request is to create a reusable RB-style skill with triggers, instructions, metadata, and resources.
An existing agent skill needs its triggers, instructions, and discovery metadata updated.
A versioned Codex and Claude Code skill must be built with instructions, metadata, validation, and references.
The user wants behavioral trigger-boundary tests with positive, negative, and repeated isolated routing trials.
The task is a with-skill versus without-skill behavioral outcome evaluation with blinded scoring and raw results.
A regression evaluation is needed for skill over-triggering with good and bad fixtures plus deterministic scoring.
Skills must be synchronized from a Git repository into the Codex skills directory using symlinks.
The versioned skills repository and its skill folders must be published and synchronized to another computer.
Selected repository skills must be copied into the Claude Code personal skills directory and verified.
An existing installation is undiscoverable and needs targeted verification and repair of links and project resources.
Global skills already exist while the repository's project resources and discovery checks need focused repair.
The task is to audit and repair stale RB resource discovery and source-of-truth configuration in an existing setup.
A new machine needs the complete RB setup workflow including global skills, project resources, visibility, and onboarding.
The repository needs the complete RB workflow from global skill installation through discovery validation and onboarding.
The user explicitly requests end-to-end RB setup, visibility verification, required project resources, and onboarding.
The request does not identify whether skills need authoring, synchronization, installation, discovery repair, or evaluation.
A brand-new climate adaptation LLM wiki must be instantiated from the template, configured, validated, and offered automations.
A fresh LLM wiki must be created from wiki-template, reidentified, validated, and prepared for first use.
A brand-new research-domain agent wiki needs configuration gathering, template instantiation, metadata updates, and validation.
New inbox files in an existing wiki must be registered, converted into reference pages, validated, and moved through intake.
Papers waiting in an existing wiki inbox require the supported registration and ingestion workflow with review flags.
Waiting source files must be ingested into an existing wiki with registry updates, reference pages, quick validation, and intake reporting.
An existing wiki needs supported nightly health checks, index and graph upkeep, and review reporting.
The task is operational wiki upkeep through linting, index rebuilding, registry inspection, and non-destructive repair reporting.
An established wiki needs its weekly maintenance workflow for graph, index, registry, and health reporting.
Updating an LLM wiki could mean ingestion, maintenance, design changes, or another wiki operation, so the intended work is unclear.
A multi-session investigation explicitly needs durable evidence, decisions, status, questions, and next actions preserved.
A context-heavy task needs durable notes so assumptions, checks, failures, and next steps survive compaction.
The long debugging and planning effort requires compact durable notes across sessions.
The project needs shared domain vocabulary, units, invariants, assumptions, and CONTEXT.md updates.
Scientific variable meanings, units, ranges, invariants, and terminology must be documented in the project context file.
An authoritative CONTEXT.md vocabulary is needed to resolve recurring confusion about domain acronyms and concepts.
The explicit tokens command is the exact trigger for current Codex token reporting.
The user asks for latest-call input tokens and current context-window usage.
The request explicitly asks for the active-session token count from Codex token-count events.
A multi-agent system needs agent boundaries, permissions, handoffs, shared state, budgets, observability, evaluation, and failure containment designed.
The multi-agent orchestration has looping and state-ownership failures that require review of handoffs, termination, traces, and recovery.
The request is to choose boundaries among deterministic tools, embedded capabilities, specialist agents, and durable orchestration.
A proposed algorithm needs novelty mapping, prior-art audit, falsifiable hypotheses, and a stop, revise, or proceed gate before planning or coding.
The scientific hypothesis must undergo literature-threat review, research-question formulation, falsifiers, and a pre-implementation gate.
The request explicitly calls for prior-art and novelty auditing with testable hypotheses before any build work.
The single-call product requirements are agreed and need ordinary implementation with structured-response and failure-behavior tests.
A single model call has unresolved contract, confidence, privacy, and failure semantics that must be discussed before implementation.
A simple single-assistant product needs a new top-level implementation plan with phases, risks, and validation.
